data.title: BofA goes neutral on Sea Limited stock on balanced risk-reward
data.description:
Bank of America downgraded Singapore-based Sea Limited (NYSE:SE) to "neutral" from a previous investment rating of "buy" on Friday, citing a more balanced risk-reward scenario after the stock's recent outperformance.

The research firm noted that SE stock is up 270% since January 2024 on the back of its improving fundamentals, compared to the S&P 500's 18% rally and Nasdaq's 20% gain for the same period. 

However, they see limited material upside in the near term for the company due to slowing macro. While still positive about its fundamentals, BofA thinks it may be difficult for Sea to consistently deliver “beat and raise” quarters.

BofA said the consensus expectations, which include 2025/26E e-commerce revenue growth of 25%/19% and EBITDA margin as a % of GMV of 0.7%/1.2%; gaming revenue growth of 16%/10% for 2025/26E with small margin improvement; and DFS (Monee) revenue growth of 29%/24%, adequately factor in Sea’s momentum.

SE's price target was unchanged at $160, implying an upside of nearly 10%. Stock is down 3.6% in early open market trading.

data.title: Cresco Labs slips after delaying Q1 earnings call
data.description:
• Shares of Cresco Labs (OTCQX:CRLBF) fell ~9% on Friday, extending a six-day losing streak after the U.S. cannabis player postponed its Q1 2025 earnings release and conference call originally scheduled for May 9, 2025.
  • As the reason for the decision, the company noted a requirement for additional time needed to complete its Q1 interim financial statements.
  • The U.S. multi-state operator said it would communicate the revised earnings call date once a final decision was made. The company reiterated its plans to submit the interim financial statements on or before the required filing date of May 30, 2025.
  • Cresco Labs (OTCQX:CRLBF) is one of the top-rated U.S.-based cannabis stocks on Wall Street. However, Seeking Alpha’s Quant System, which consistently beats the market, rates CRLBF as a Hold.

data.title: Vestis gains amid takeover speculation
data.description:
Vestis (NYSE:VSTS) quickly gained 4.3% amid some takeover speculation for the uniform rental company. 

Vestis (NYSE:VSTS) may have attracted a new round of takeover speculation, according to traders, who cited a Betaville "uncooked" alert that was circulating on Friday. 

The company that's speculated to be interested in Vestis (NYSE:VSTS) may be considering a hostile takeover of the firm, according to the report, which cited people following the matter.

The speculation comes after Vestis (VSTS) on Tuesday appointed Jim Barber as president and chief executive officer. 

French competitor Elis (OTCPK:ELSSF) in early October said it ended discussions with Vestis (VSTS) and US peer UniFirst (UNF) over possible takeover offers. Reuters reported in December that private equity firms Advent International and Apollo Global (APO) were exploring a potential acquisition  of Vestis (VSTS). 

Vestis (VSTS) didn't immediately respond to Seeking Alpha's email request for comment.

data.title: MeiraGTx gets FDA RMAT status for its Parkinson’s disease treatment
data.description:
• MeiraGTx (NASDAQ:MGTX) said on Friday that the U.S. FDA has granted regenerative medicine advanced therapy (RMAT) designation to AAV-GAD, its Parkinson’s disease treatment.
  • The RMAT designation is usually granted for cellular, genetic or other biologic therapies and entails benefits such as early interactions with the FDA to discuss surrogate or intermediate endpoints that could support the accelerated approval and meet post-approval requirements.
  • This status was awarded after positive data from 3 clinical studies, the company said.

data.title: Kraft Heinz asks suppliers for 60-days' notice for coffee price hikes - Reuters
data.description:
Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC) is asking its coffee suppliers to provide the company with 60-days’ notice before raising prices, according to an internal document viewed by Reuters.

The parent company of Maxwell House, McCafé, Gevalia, and Yuban buys most of its raw coffee beans sourced from tropical regions including Brazil, Vietnam, Colombia, and Indonesia, all of which are subject to U.S. trade tariffs of at least 10%. And as contracts between the grower and buyer stipulate the buyer is responsible for import fees at the market of destination, Kraft Heinz could be forced to absorb the higher costs imposed on its coffee suppliers. 

According to the document cited by Reuters sent in April to its suppliers, Kraft Heinz (NASDAQ:KHC) asked for prices to be raised only if tariffs are permanent and to immediately reverse them if they are removed.

Faced with a price sensitive consumer, Kraft (NASDAQ:KHC) must tread lightly when considering higher prices for a beverage enjoyed by 66% of Americans. As a result of severe droughts in Brazil and massive flooding in Vietnam (both of which account for 56% of global coffee supply), the price of a pound of coffee is expected to increase by at least 20% this year—excluding any impact on import tariffs. This puts the cost of a cup of home-brewed coffee at $0.22, up from $0.18 per cup at the beginning of the year.

And while coffee represents just 3% of Kraft Heinz’s (NASDAQ:KHC) total business, it brought in $835M in revenue in 2024, according to the company's latest annual report.
